note: A-verbs (M5, parity-checklist §A — third-pass rulings 2026-07-03/3) — the model verbs become reachable from unfold with MINIMAL, HIDDEN-BY-DEFAULT affordances (Chris's ruled design constraint: match unfold's sleek/clean language — no toolbar, no persistent chrome). Three layers: (1) a PURE applicability gate ufVerbAllowed(verb, s) — which verb is invocable given the selection shape (card/group sel · wire · clipboard non-empty · model emptiness) — acceptance-proven red-then-green so the menu can never offer an impossible verb; (2) the nodes module becomes the single owner of the remaining inline model mutations: edge label/reverse/delete and node kind/desc mutations move OUT of inspector.ts's inline handlers into named NodesApi verbs, and the footer clear-all body moves OUT of main.ts into NodesApi.clearAll — one code path per verb regardless of surface (files.loadMmdText precedent), with the legacy inspector/footer rewired as pure triggers, behaviour unchanged; (3) unfold grows the hidden affordances: overlay-scoped keyboard shortcuts (Delete/Backspace delete · ⌘C copy · ⌘V paste · ⌘D duplicate · ⌘Z/⇧⌘Z undo/redo — suppressed while typing in inputs/textareas/contenteditable, and Esc precedence stays with ufEscAction) and a slim '⋯' actions menu that exists ONLY while a card or wire is selected (in the inspector header), offering exactly the ufVerbAllowed-gated verbs: add node · connect · duplicate · copy · paste · wrap in group · edit kind/desc · edge label/reverse/delete · delete · clear all · undo/redo. Every verb bridges unfold's single selection to the shared model selection first (deps.selection.selectOnly(spec.sel)), invokes the module verb, then rebuilds the universe (refreshFromModel — the io/mermaid precedent). CONNECT is the one two-step verb: invoke on the selected source card, click a target card to create the edge (nodes.makeEdge), Esc cancels. Assumptions recorded (amend if Chris rules otherwise): (1) SELECT-ALL is deferred to backlog with multi-select (unfold is single-selection; the verb has no meaning here yet) — ruled 2026-07-03/3; (2) paste places at the model's default position (clipboard.pasteClip(null)) — unfold has no pointer-world yet (that lands with the drag plan); (3) wrap-in-group on a single selection wraps that one node (legal in the model verb today); (4) undo/redo are always offered — an empty stack is already a safe no-op in the history engine; (5) add node creates a rect at the model default via nodes.addNode('rect', null, null, {}) then reveals + selects it in unfold — shape choice is legacy chrome, not ported (the 9-shape toolbar stays legacy; kind, not shape, is the architectural attribute); (6) clear-all keeps a confirm() at each caller (destructive), the model mutation itself lives in nodes.clearAll; (7) edge verbs take the edge id (DiagramEdge.id — edges are identity-bearing in the model). Execution order: AFTER m5-p-tabs2 (this plan's initUnfold signature is cumulative on it: deps gain nodes, clipboard, history on top of slice, theming). Resume: npm run novakai:status -- --plan docs/novakai/plans/m5-a-verbs.plan.json · prove behaviour: npm run novakai:acceptance -- --plan docs/novakai/plans/m5-a-verbs.plan.json · runtime criteria (browser, 0-context verify): (1) with nothing selected, unfold shows ZERO new chrome (hidden by default); (2) selecting a card reveals the '⋯' menu; delete removes the card everywhere (unfold + legacy + serialised text — one model); (3) ⌘C/⌘V round-trips a card copy, ⌘D duplicates, Delete deletes, ⌘Z undoes it and ⇧⌘Z redoes — all without opening the menu; (4) connect: invoke on card A, click card B, the new edge appears in the wire layer and the serialised text; Esc mid-connect cancels cleanly; (5) selecting a wire offers label/reverse/delete — reverse flips the arrow in the serialised text, the legacy edge inspector shows the same result (one code path); (6) edit kind/desc from unfold changes the map text and the reading inspector; (7) clear-all asks for confirmation, then empties the model on both surfaces; undo restores it; (8) typing in the browse search or mermaid textarea never triggers a verb shortcut; (9) zero console errors through the whole sequence.

phases.title: Pure verb gate
phases.subtitle: ufVerbAllowed: verb applicability from the selection shape — acceptance-tested red before code

phases.title: One owner per mutation
phases.subtitle: nodes gains setEdgeLabel/reverseEdge/deleteEdge/setNodeMeta/clearAll; inspector and main.ts rewire as triggers, behaviour unchanged

phases.title: Hidden affordances in unfold
phases.subtitle: overlay-scoped shortcuts + the selection-only '⋯' actions menu + connect mode; every verb bridges selection, invokes the module verb, rebuilds

intent.problem: Which verbs are invocable depends on the selection shape (card or group selected · wire selected · clipboard state · model emptiness). Scattered as if-guards inside a menu painter this is unprovable DOM-bound branching — and an offered-but-impossible verb (paste with an empty clipboard, edge ops with no wire) is exactly the class of silent wrongness the loop exists to kill.
intent.approach: One pure function ufVerbAllowed(verb, s) → boolean over s = { sel: string | null; wire: boolean; clipboard: boolean; modelEmpty: boolean } in a dependency-free file src/panel/unfold-verbs.ts (ufDockReduce precedent). Table: addNode/undo/redo → always; connect/copy/duplicate/wrap/editMeta → a card or group is selected; delete → a card/group OR a wire is selected; paste → the clipboard is non-empty; edgeLabel/edgeReverse/edgeDelete → a wire is selected; clearAll → the model is non-empty; any unknown verb → false (fail closed).
intent.rationale: E2/H1 factor-to-pure: the actions menu and the shortcut handler are dumb painters/triggers of this gate, so 'the menu can never offer an impossible verb' is a red-then-green acceptance claim, not prose.

fm.name: ufVerbAllowed
fm.description: pure verb-applicability gate for unfold's hidden actions: over { sel, wire, clipboard, modelEmpty } — addNode/undo/redo always; connect/copy/duplicate/wrap/editMeta need a selected card or group; delete needs a card/group or a wire; paste needs a non-empty clipboard; edgeLabel/edgeReverse/edgeDelete need a selected wire; clearAll needs a non-empty model; unknown verbs are refused (fail closed)

interfaces.name: ufVerbAllowed
accepts: s: { sel: string | null; wire: boolean; clipboard: boolean; modelEmpty: boolean }

cases.name: addNode is always available (even on an empty model)
delete works on a selected card
delete works on a selected wire
paste with clipboard content is available with nothing selected
edge verbs need a selected wire, not a card
edge label is available on a selected wire
clearAll is refused on an empty model
undo is always offered (empty stack is the engine's safe no-op)
an unknown verb is refused (fail closed)

intent.problem: Three model mutations still live inline in chrome handlers: edge label/reverse/delete and node kind edits inside inspector.ts's render closures, and the whole clear-all body inside main.ts's footer onclick. Unfold cannot reach them without duplicating mutation + sync + history logic — the exact drift novakai exists to kill.
intent.approach: NodesApi gains the named verbs: setEdgeLabel(id, label), reverseEdge(id) (swap from/to, drop manual bend/labelPos — a reversed route re-anchors), deleteEdge(id), setNodeMeta(id, patch: { kind?, desc? }), and clearAll() (empty nodes/edges/hier, reset counters, clear selection — the main.ts body verbatim; confirm() stays at each caller). Each verb performs the mutation and fires the same hooks the inline code fired today (render/sync/reroute where applicable, pushHistory on commit). Edge verbs resolve edges by DiagramEdge.id.
intent.rationale: One owner per mutation regardless of surface: the legacy inspector/footer and unfold's menu become two triggers of the same proven behaviour, and every §A row maps to a named, typed verb.

fm.name: initNodes
fm.description: node + edge model verbs bound to ctx: addNode, makeEdge, deleteSelection, alignNodes, wrapInGroup, bringToFront, and the factored single-owner mutations — setEdgeLabel, reverseEdge (swaps endpoints, drops manual bend/labelPos), deleteEdge (by DiagramEdge.id), setNodeMeta (kind/desc patch), clearAll (empty model + counters + selection; confirmation belongs to callers); each verb fires the same render/sync/history hooks its former inline body fired

intent.problem: NodesApi's public surface stops at bringToFront — the factored verbs have no typed door.
intent.approach: Widen the interface: setEdgeLabel(id: string, label: string): void; reverseEdge(id: string): void; deleteEdge(id: string): void; setNodeMeta(id: string, patch: { kind?: NodeKind | null; desc?: string }): void; clearAll(): void.
intent.rationale: The composition root passes nodes into unfold's deps; the type is the contract the map gates.

fm.name: NodesApi
fm.description: public surface: addNode, makeEdge, deleteSelection, alignNodes, wrapInGroup, bringToFront, setEdgeLabel, reverseEdge, deleteEdge, setNodeMeta, clearAll

intent.problem: renderEdgeInspector and renderSingleInspector mutate the model inline (e.label = …, edges.splice, n.kind = …) — after the factoring these would be a second code path for the same verbs.
intent.approach: The inline mutation bodies delegate to the nodes verbs (setEdgeLabel, reverseEdge, deleteEdge, setNodeMeta); the inspector keeps owning its DOM, field wiring and live-input cadence. Behaviour is unchanged — the legacy inspector becomes a pure trigger.
intent.rationale: Two triggers, one code path (the P-panel files precedent) — the legacy surface stays intact as Chris's reference without owning any mutation the new surface also needs.

fm.name: initInspector
fm.description: build the inspector bound to ctx, nodes and selection; edge and node-meta mutations delegate to the nodes module's single-owner verbs (setEdgeLabel, reverseEdge, deleteEdge, setNodeMeta) — the inspector owns DOM and cadence, never the mutation

intent.problem: The footer clear-all onclick owns the whole model-emptying body inline — the last §A mutation living in chrome.
intent.approach: The handler keeps its confirm() and delegates the mutation to nodes.clearAll().
intent.rationale: Same single-owner rule; the destructive confirmation stays at the surface where the click happens.

fm.name: bind toolbar DOM
fm.description: bind shape tools, link, undo/redo, tidy, export, save, zoom and help controls; the footer clear-all keeps its confirm() and delegates the mutation to nodes.clearAll

intent.problem: Unfold is a read-only surface for every §A verb except rename and frontmatter: no way to add, connect, delete, duplicate, edit kind/desc, drive edge ops, undo/redo or clear — the last body of legacy-only capability blocking the deletion gate's model-verb rows.
intent.approach: Hidden by default, revealed by selection (the ruled design language): (1) overlay-scoped keyboard shortcuts — Delete/Backspace delete, ⌘C copy, ⌘V paste, ⌘D duplicate, ⌘Z/⇧⌘Z undo/redo — suppressed while an input/textarea/contenteditable has focus; Esc precedence stays with ufEscAction (connect-cancel joins its highest-priority tier); (2) a slim '⋯' actions button in the inspector header that exists ONLY while a card or wire is selected, opening a minimal menu of exactly the ufVerbAllowed-gated verbs (add node · connect · duplicate · copy · paste · wrap in group · edit kind/desc · edge label/reverse/delete · delete · clear all · undo · redo); (3) connect mode — invoked on the selected source, the next card click creates the edge via nodes.makeEdge, Esc cancels; (4) every verb bridges spec.sel → deps.selection.selectOnly first, invokes the module verb (nodes/clipboard/history — never an inline mutation), then rebuilds via refreshFromModel (the io/mermaid precedent). deps widen to { selection, camera, files, mermaid, slice, theming, nodes, clipboard, history } — cumulative on m5-p-tabs2; main.ts builds all of them before unfold.
intent.rationale: The gate is proven pure logic, the module verbs are single-owner, and unfold's chrome stays a dumb trigger layer — zero new chrome when nothing is selected, per the ruled constraint. Kind (not shape) is the architectural attribute, so add-node ports the verb without the 9-shape legacy toolbar.

fm.name: initUnfold
fm.description: build the primary surface (own DOM + own CSS, the planner isolation pattern) bound to ctx; reads ctx.state + ctx.bodies, writes only through the shared hooks path; the dock carries a temporary legacy-compare affordance instead of a ✕ (M4 correction); the panel is a real dock driven by the pure ufDockReduce — five tabs on a two-row strip (reveal · io · mermaid / slice · style), chevron collapse, drag resize, state persisted under unfold.dock; model verbs are reachable through hidden-by-default affordances — overlay-scoped shortcuts and a selection-only '⋯' actions menu gated by the pure ufVerbAllowed, every verb bridging the unfold selection to the shared model selection and invoking the single-owner module verbs (nodes/clipboard/history) before a full rebuild

interfaces.name: initUnfold
accepts: deps: { selection: SelectionApi; camera: CameraApi; files: FilesApi; mermaid: MermaidApi; slice: SliceApi; theming: ThemingApi; nodes: NodesApi; clipboard: ClipboardApi; history: HistoryApi }
